title: Creative Director
description: Designity is a Creative-as-a-Service (CaaS) platform redefining how brands scale their creative and marketing efforts and teams. We connect brands with top creative and marketing experts, guided by experienced Creative Directors and powered by a collaborative platform that delivers fast, high-impact results. Trusted by brands like Calendly, Kung Fu Tea, and Marriott, Designity offers a more cost-efficient and flexible alternative to expensive agencies and in-house teams - while giving top creatives the environment to do their best work. As a Creative Director , you’ll lead work across the full creative spectrum, from striking visual identities and graphics to motion design, web experiences, and immersive digital campaigns. You’ll guide a talented team to craft work that not only looks amazing but drives real impact. You’ll turn business goals into emotionally compelling storytelling, sharpen ideas with strategic direction, and create space for creativity to thrive. Using modern AI tools, you’ll accelerate ideation while protecting the craft that makes every project exceptional. If you’re energized by shaping bold ideas, mentoring creative teams, and leading client conversations with clarity and style, this role is made for you. Your role: Lead the creative vision for multiple clients across diverse industries, from brand platforms and integrated campaigns to storytelling and digital content. Turn complex business goals into powerful, emotionally resonant creative concepts. Guide and mentor talented creatives, ensuring every project meets high standards of craft and impact. Present and defend creative ideas with confidence and clarity in client conversations. Foster an environment where originality, curiosity, and collaboration thrive. Use AI tools to enhance ideation, streamline workflows, and free up space for deep creative work. What you bring: 8 years of experience in the creative field, including 3+ years in creative leadership roles. Proven ability to inspire and lead multidisciplinary teams toward bold, brand-defining creative work. Deep strategic understanding of branding, storytelling, and visual communication. Strong focus on connecting creativity with measurable business impact. Great communication and presentation skills, able to sell ideas, align stakeholders, and guide feedback cycles effectively. Experienced in delivering high-quality creative work across branding, web and digital design, motion graphics, and integrated marketing campaigns. Fluent in English, highly adaptable, and self-driven in a remote environment. Passionate about integrating AI tools to enhance creativity, efficiency, and innovation. As we partner closely with clients across the U.S., we ask for at least 6 hours of overlap with U.S. Eastern Time (EST) to make collaboration easy and effective What You’ll Enjoy: Collaboration with top-tier creatives and ambitious brands Working remotely in a vibrant virtual office with exceptional talent from around the world Paid PTO to relax and recharge Professional growth opportunities and ongoing skill development A fast-moving, supportive team environment To be considered for this role, please complete your application at designity.com/careers/creative-director Our team reviews every submission carefully and will contact candidates who best fit the role within 2–3 weeks . Thank you for your interest in Designity. We truly appreciate the time and effort you put into applying. At Designity, employment decisions are based on business needs, qualifications, and merit. We’re an Equal Opportunity Employer that values diversity in backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences; it’s what makes our team and our work stronger. Be cautious when applying. Official communications from Designity will come only from the Designity.com domain. We will never request payment details. Beware of scammers using fake domains like Designity.team to attempt fraud.
score_reason: Senior CD role with full creative scope and AI integration, but no salary posted, no explicit remote confirmation, and no location/timezone clarity are notable gaps.

title: Design Director
description: Design Director Remote Our client is seeking a Design Director to serve as the creative North Star and operational backbone of our design team for a DTC health and wellness brand. This is a high-impact role for a leader who is as passionate about mentorship and process as they are about world-class visual aesthetics. You will be responsible for ensuring a unified "look and feel" across all brand touchpoints, co-owning our Brand Guidelines, and optimizing the systems, specifically ClickUp and our DAM, that empower our designers to do their best work. Core Responsibilities Team Mentorship: Lead, coach, and inspire a team of designers. Conduct regular creative reviews and 1:1s to elevate the craft and career growth of the team. Brand Guardianship: In partnership with the Brand Team, co-own and evolve the official Brand Guidelines. Ensure every piece of output is aligned with our visual identity. Design Approvals: Act as the final "eyes" on high-priority projects, ensuring quality, consistency, and strategic alignment before work goes live. Operational Excellence: Own and organize the DAM (Digital Asset Management) system to ensure assets are accessible, tagged correctly, and up-to-date. Workflow Optimization: Monitor and refine our ClickUp environment. While you aren’t a dedicated Project Manager, you will help design and implement the workflows that keep the team efficient and on deadline. Hero Projects: Lead the design for high-stakes initiatives, new brand launches, or complex creative problems that require a veteran hand. System Building: Develop templates, UI kits, and design patterns that the wider team can use to scale their output. Standard Setting: Dive into the tools (Figma, Adobe Suite, etc.) to set the visual "bar" for the team to follow. Requirements & Qualifications Experience: 8+ years in design, with 3+ years in a leadership or management capacity. Experience with CPG DTC strongly preferred Portfolio: A strong portfolio demonstrating a mastery of brand systems, typography, and multi-channel campaigns. Systems Fluency: Deep familiarity with ClickUp (or similar PM tools) and the ability to build/optimize project workflows. Asset Management: Experience managing or implementing a DAM system to keep a growing library of assets organized. The "Coach" Mentality: You find more professional fulfillment in your team’s success than in your own individual accolades. Strategic Communication: You can articulate the why behind design decisions to non-design stakeholders and executives. Process-Oriented: You believe that a great creative output is the result of a great creative process. Cross-functional Collaboration : Proven ability to partner with diver teams (e.g. Brand, Performance Creative, Sales) to deliver cohesive, business-aligned results. Design: Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign). Project Management: ClickUp (Admin-level familiarity is a huge plus). Organization: Experience with DAM platforms (e.g., Pics.io, Brandfolder, Bynder etc)

title: Creative Director
description: Who we are: Four years ago, Dragonfruit set out to bring together some of the most talented visual storytellers, viral video strategists, and big-name clients, to change the way we create and consume on YouTube. The result has been bigger than we could have imagined, having generated over a billion organic views and hundreds of millions of subscribers for creators like MrBeast, Ali Abdaal, Mark Manson, Chris Williamson, Codie Sanchez, and orgs like Y Combinator, e.l.f beauty, WHOOP, ClickUp, Center for Humane Technology & more. Unlike other social media agencies or YouTube businesses, Dragonfruit refuses to celebrate a “creator economy” that trades viewers’ time and attention for rapid-fire dopamine entertainment. Instead, we want to lead a global movement to make YouTube a more valuable and educational place for all people; where views and subscribers are never an end in and of themselves, but rather a means of increasing human flourishing; where viral-educational YouTube channels not only bolster awareness and impact for our client's brands, but also create genuine value in viewers’ lives. Dragonfruit's Mission Dragonfruit exists to help the world’s most important ideas travel farther. We partner with experts, educators, scientists, founders, and creators to turn deep knowledge into media people actually want to watch, share, and act on. In a world where attention is often optimized for noise, we’re building a company that rewards clarity, substance, and human impact — combining creative judgment, production craft, and AI-powered systems to help better information reach more people. Our mission is to build the creative infrastructure for a healthier, more thoughtful internet. The Role It's clear that the YouTube Creative Director is quickly becoming one of the most coveted roles in digital media. And at Dragonfruit, they are the life source of the business, spending their days bringing to life our agency’s core product: viral-educational long-form videos. As a Dragonfruit YouTube Creative Director, you are truly the face of the company, building close 1:1 creative partnerships with our high-profile and high net-worth clients, operating confidently as the primary strategist, artist, and coach behind their YouTube channels. Day-to-day you will collaborate with YouTube Producers, Post-Production Managers, video editors, and thumbnail designers, and consult with other Creative Directors and team leads, to design, build, and optimize powerful, educational YouTube brands. From competitor research, to brand positioning, art direction, concept ideation, title/thumbnail design, script optimization, final video edit reviews, studying analytics, running a/b tests, and swapping out packaging after publication, you will have the opportunity to create life-changing growth for your clients and their viewers. What you can expect: An exciting startup culture – work cross-functionally, wear several hats, and occasionally work long hours, during evenings, and/or weekends. We are a hungry and ambitious group of people trying to accomplish something large and challenging and we’re looking for folks who are enthusiastic and willing to do what is required to reach their goals. Lift up others – provide constructive mentorship and feedback to video editors, thumbnail designers, and scriptwriters to align with client expectations and optimize video performance. Lift up yourself – use Dragonfruit’s culture of excellence as an incubator to improve your professional skills, learn new mental models, sharpen your critical thinking, and operate at the forefront of new media. Be a key player in Dragonfruit’ s growth – help take the company from 40 employees to 100+, identifying blind spots in our service offering and working collaboratively with leadership to improve systems/processes Earn more – take advantage of regular performance-based raises, generous referral and sales commissions, cash bonuses for video performance & more Skills and Qualifications: P

Lead Concept and Verbal Excellence Lead creative development from brief through production across multiple accounts, teams, and workstreams. Review, edit, and elevate concepts and copy—from campaign platforms, naming, and voice systems to headlines, scripts, social, digital experiences, and long-form storytelling. Protect a clear brand voice and conceptual throughline as ideas adapt across channels, formats, audiences, and moments. Partner with strategy, account leadership, media, production, technology, and project management to make ambitious ideas real. Inspire and Develop Talent Mentor Associate Creative Directors, Senior Art Directors, Senior Copywriters, and emerging creative leaders. Build high-performing teams through clear expectations, regular feedback, coaching, and opportunities to grow. Help identify, recruit, onboard, and retain exceptional creative talent and trusted external partners. Model curiosity, accountability, resilience, and respect—especially when timelines are tight and the work is still evolving. Build Client Confidence Serve as a senior creative advisor who understands the client’s business, brand, audience, and competitive landscape. Frame, present, and sell ideas with a compelling narrative, strategic rationale, and confidence about how they will come to life. Navigate feedb

This posting is not tied to a current open role, but is an opportunity to join our talent pool for future positions. title: Vice President of Marketing & Communications | Global Brand Growth | Beauty & Wellness
description: Vice President of Marketing & Communications | Global Brand Growth | Beauty & Wellness Shape the Global Voice of Waterless Beauty with Oliveda Waterless Beauty is the fastest-growing sector in the entire beauty industry, and we are the fastest-growing waterless beauty brand disrupting the industry. Oliveda is redefining the future of beauty through the power of waterless formulations and the unmatched potency of olive tree skincare science. We are not simply creating another skincare brand. We are building a global movement. Through our unique Tree-to-Beauty philosophy , powered by 3,000-year-old olive tree skincare science, Oliveda is creating a new standard for beauty, one that is highly effective, sustainable, and transformative. Our formulations replace up to 70% of industrial water with phytonutrient-rich antioxidants, delivering clinically proven results and a fundamentally different approach to skincare. We are seeking an exceptional Vice President of Marketing & Communications to define, lead, and scale Oliveda’s global brand vision. This is a high-impact executive opportunity for a strategic marketing leader who has successfully built premium consumer brands, accelerated DTC growth, created powerful brand narratives, and led high-performing teams in fast-growth environments. As a member of the Executive Leadership Team , the Vice President of Marketing & Communications will shape company strategy, brand direction, consumer engagement, and global growth initiatives, helping establish Oliveda as the global category leader in Waterless Beauty. The Opportunity The Vice President of Marketing & Communications will own the strategy and execution behind how consumers discover, experience, and connect with Oliveda worldwide. This executive leader will build and scale a world-class marketing organization responsible for: Global brand strategy and positioning Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) growth eCommerce acceleration Customer acquisition and retention CRM and lifecycle marketing Communications and storytelling Social media and community growth Integrated campaigns Global market expansion This role requires a visionary marketing executive who combines creativity with data-driven decision-making and understands how to build emotional connections between consumers and purpose-driven brands. What Makes Oliveda Unique? Oliveda is more than Waterless Beauty. We are a movement. title: Directeur·trice de création | Creative Director
description: Directeur·trice de création La vie chez Bloom Rejoignez l’équipe dynamique de Bloom, une agence numérique innovante avec des bureaux à Montréal et Toronto. Ici, on place la flexibilité, le bien-être de nos employés et l’équilibre entre vie professionnelle et personnelle au cœur de nos priorités. Depuis 19 ans, nous sommes fiers d’être reconnus comme l’un des meilleurs employeurs au Canada. Avec plus de 75 spécialistes passionnés, nous aidons les marques à se démarquer en ligne grâce à une approche marketing 360. Bloom offre un modèle de travail hybride, et nos entrevues se déroulent par vidéoconférence. Certains postes peuvent nécessiter des rencontres en personne. Aperçu du rôle Bloom accélère sa croissance en déployant un produit créatif ancré à la fois dans la performance et l'excellence artisanale (craft). Nous sommes à la recherche d'un·e directeur·trice de création pour définir et guider cette évolution. Il s'agit d'un rôle de leadership clé dans lequel vous façonnerez le modèle créatif, l'équipe et le travail lui-même. Vous comprenez comment les idées voyagent à travers la culture et comment les outils émergents transforment la production créative. Vous définirez la présence de nos clients sur toutes les plateformes, ancrerez les campagnes dans des idées fortes et veillerez à ce que notre production créative capte l'attention du public cible tout en générant des résultats mesurables. Cela inclut l'intégration responsable et intentionnelle d'outils génératifs et intelligents pour renforcer à la fois l'art de la création et la performance. Responsabilités clés Vision créative et excellence de l'exécution (Craftsmanship) : Assumer la responsabilité des standards créatifs pour l'ensemble des projets clients, en dirigeant le développement des idées de campagne, des systèmes de contenu, de la direction artistique et de la conception-rédaction. Agir en tant qu'autorité créative finale pour garantir une qualité irréprochable et réduire les cycles de révision. Résultats axés sur la performance : Propulser un contenu créatif optimisé pour des plateformes comme Meta, TikTok et Google. Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les équipes de médias payants et de stratégie, en prônant une approche d'expérimentation (test-and-learn) pour préserver l'intégrité créative tout en maximisant les résultats mesurables. Intégration de l'IA générative : Apporter une expérience pratique des outils génératifs (image, vidéo, texte) afin d'enrichir l'idéation et la production. Collaborer avec la direction de l'agence pour établir des directives claires d'utilisation responsable, naviguer les questions éthiques et encadrer l'équipe dans une application efficace et critique. Leadership client et d'équipe : Présenter et vendre le travail avec clarté aux parties prenantes de direction, en instaurant une relation de confiance et en guidant les clients vers des décisions créatives plus audacieuses. Gérer, diriger, encadrer et perfectionner les designers et les créateurs, en élevant la barre tant pour l'aspect visuel que pour la rédaction au sein de toute l'équipe. Bâtir le moteur créatif : Contribuer à optimiser et à concevoir des systèmes évolutifs et structurés (briefing, idéation, assurance qualité) qui équilibrent l'ambition créative et la faisabilité technique. Participer à l'évolution globale du produit créatif et du positionnement de Bloom. Connaissances et compétences Un·e leader créatif·ve accompli·e possédant de 8 à 12 ans (ou plus) d'expérience en agence et un portfolio démontrant l'excellence dans le développement de concepts, le récit de marque (storytelling), la direction artistique et la conception-rédaction. Aussi à l'aise pour discuter de vision créative, d'objectifs d'affaires que de détails d'exécution, avec la capacité de lier ces trois éléments pour créer un travail à impact mesurable. Expérience dans la mise en place et l'amélioration de systèmes créatifs, de flux de travail (workflows) et de normes de qualité permettant aux équipe
score_reason: Senior CD role in Canada with creative leadership scope, but hybrid model (not 100% remote) and no USD compensation listed are notable gaps.

Community. Design and pilot a named community program, pop-up events, and a city-ambassador model — the layer that turns audience into belonging, and belonging into commerce. The department. The outcome we promise, real relief and better living, is not visually self-evident the way a fashion or shapewear product is. A shapewear brand can let the product speak for itself; we cannot. Our brand has to build belief through claim, mechanism, and story, and it has to do that within compliance. A strong fashion or product-led track record does not automatically transfer to this problem. You will own brand positioning, narrative, voice, and identity, and the vision extends into product itself: digital product, including claims and UX and UI at a directional level, physical product and the packaging experience, social media, and the UGC that increasingly carries our brands. This is brand and product vision that performs and sells, not brand for its own sake. What You Will Own Brand foundation. Steering social media presence and the UGC engine so the brand shows up consistently and credibly where customers actually are. Brand meets performance. Partnering with performance and creative so brand and direct response reinforce each other, and claims are built and tested responsibly and compliantly. Team and brand health. Building and leading the brand team over time, and tracking brand health so the customer's perception informs how we build. Requirements What We Are Looking For A senior brand or brand-and-product leader for DTC consumer brands, ideally health, wellness, supplements, or other claims-led categories, with the maturity to build and lead a team. A track record building brand and product vision from a low base: positioning, messaging, identity, and the connection into product, packaging, and social, not just maintaining an existing brand book. A real grasp of claims-led health and wellness: how to build belief through claim and mechanism within compliance, and why a strong fashion or product-led track record does not automatically transfer. Comfort operating inside a performance and direct-response environment, where brand is expected to drive conversion, not only to build awareness. Native or near-native English, with the editorial and aesthetic judgment to own brand voice for a US audience and to represent the brand to US media and partners. Nice to Have Experience in health and wellness, supplements, or the 55-plus demographic, a creator or influencer ecosystem background, packaging and product-marketing experience, and experience launching new brands from zero. Benefits What We Offer A fully remote leadership position with real ownership over a function you will build and lead, and direct visibility with the founders. A monthly performance bonus tied to your KPIs, on top of base. Paid time off.

Who we are Squido Studio is a small, remote-first Québec studio of unapologetic game lovers and VR believers. We've shipped titles like No More Rainbows , DigiGods , and Wizherd , and we run a flat, no-BS shop where transparency is our #1 value , we say things as they are, we don't do ego or politics, and everyone's ideas get heard. The art vision We want a Breath of the Wild -inspired art style , vibrant, stylized, cel-shaded, and timeless. Strong readable silhouettes, saturated painterly palettes, soft atmospheric depth, charming character and creature design, and a fully zero-UI, diegetic presentation where the world tells the story. The catch that makes this role special: it all has to look gorgeous and run flawlessly at 72/90fps on standalone VR . In VR, performance is part of the art, and nailing a BotW-caliber look within that budget is exactly the challenge we're hiring for. What you'll own The visual vision. Define and champion the art direction end-to-end, style, color, lighting, character/creature design, environments, VFX, and the zero-UI diegetic language. Direct music and sound design (in-house and with our audio partners) so the game sounds as intentional as it looks, spatial audio is core to our zero-UI design. Leading the team. Grow and lead a cross-disciplinary art + audio team (concept, 3D environment/character/enemy, VFX, technical art, animation, UI, audio). Set the bar, give sharp and generous feedback, and create an environment where artists do their best work. Beauty within budget. Partner with our Technical Director to hit a stylized visual benchmark that survives standalone-VR constraints, silhouette clarity, stepped toon shading, rim lighting, painterly albedo, baked GI, LODs, and draw-call discipline. Cross-functional partnership. Work hand-in-hand with the Lead Game Designer and engineering to take features from graybox to final quality, and defend a cohesive visual identity across the whole game. Iterate relentlessly. Use playtest feedback to sharpen readability, comfort, and impact, and kill your darlings when the game needs it. What you bring 6+ years in game art , with shipped titles and real time leading/directing an art team. A portfolio that proves strong stylized / non-photorealistic art direction , you can define a cohesive, readable, characterful look (cel-shading, toon/NPR, painterly stylization à la BotW) and hold a team to it. Deep understanding of real-time art performance , you think in draw calls, overdraw, LODs, baked vs. dynamic lighting, and shader cost, not just pretty frames. Leadership people actually want to follow , clear direction, generous feedback, zero ego. You lift the team up rather than gatekeeping. Hands-on fluency , you can jump into the engine/DCC tools to prototype a look, set up a shader/lighting pass, and show rather than just tell. Comfort directing audio (or a strong track record collaborating closely with audio leads/composers). Fluent, clear communication in a collaborative, remote team. Bonus points VR / XR shipped experience (Quest or PCVR) and an instinct for VR comfort, scale, and legibility. Experience with Unity and stylized/NPR rendering pipelines (URP, custom toon shaders, Application SpaceWarp). A love of the Nintendo of art direction, timeless over trendy, clarity over clutter. Enthusiasm for AI-assisted workflows as a force multiplier (we lean into them). Understand and/or speaking french. title: Principal AI Creative Director, POD
description: Description Advertising is being remade. The brands that win the next decade will be the ones that figure out how to deliver creative that is personal, performant, and produced at a scale no human team can match alone. Generative AI is the unlock. But generation alone is not the advantage. The advantage belongs to the system that connects creation to delivery to learning, and gets better with every impression. Amazon Ads is building that system. We are investing in the models, the infrastructure, and the product intelligence to make personalized, brand-aligned creative a default capability for every advertiser, not a luxury reserved for the largest budgets. The work is early, the stakes are high, and the opportunity to define how this category develops is real. We are building that system. We need a Principal AI Creative Director to define what great looks like inside it and to make that definition teachable, scalable, and machine-readable. Key job responsibilities As Principal AI Creative Director for Personalization Optimization and Design (POD), you are the human judgment layer at the center of Amazon's AI creative system. Generative AI has commoditized asset creation. The durable differentiator is now creative direction: the taste, the standards, the brand intelligence, and the systematic thinking that ensures our system produces creative that builds brands, not just optimizes clicks. This is not a role about using AI tools, though you must be among the most fluent practitioners working today. It is about understanding what makes a great commercial, a great piece of content, a great brand moment, and then decomposing that understanding into primitives: the story arc, the color logic, the compositional tension, the emotional beat, the brand signal. Those primitives are what our systems learn from, replicate, and eventually govern at scale. You will operate as a principal-level individual contributor with org-wide influence, partnering directly with design, engineering, science, and product leadership across every surface (POD) owns. A day in the life Creative Direction And Quality Standards Define and own the creative quality bar for AI-generated assets across POD's full portfolio, including images, video, copy, and multi-format compositions. Establish brand intelligence frameworks that ensure AI outputs are aesthetically coherent, emotionally resonant, and brand-safe at scale. Act as the senior creative editor for AI-generated content, distinguishing exceptional work from "good enough" outputs and articulating the difference in decomposable, teachable terms. Decomposing Creative into Primitives. Lead the Systemization of Taste: Break down what makes great creative into its constituent elements: narrative structure, color theory, compositional hierarchy, emotional arc, pacing, and brand signal. Translate these primitives into structured frameworks, including prompt libraries, creative briefs, quality rubrics, and annotation schemas, that inform model training, DCO reward signals, and creative tooling. Define the primitives that power aesthetic moderation: the signals and thresholds that allow our systems to evaluate, filter, and rank AI-generated content without requiring human review of every asset. Partner with applied science and engineering to operationalize these as machine-readable inputs. Hands-On Production And Prototyping Produce high-quality creative content independently across modalities, including image, video, and copy, using the leading AI creation tools. Bring that same hands-on fluency to prototyping: build working creative prototypes that demonstrate new customer experiences, surface trade-offs, and create decision points for design, product, and engineering leadership. This role operates in a demo-driven culture where prototypes move decisions. You are expected to contribute directly to that culture, not just direct others within it. Maintain current, deep fluency across tools including text-to-image (Mid

Architectural Efficiency: Minimizing token usage/latency while increasing output quality. Brand Alignment: Scoring of agent outputs against specific brand guidelines using automated evaluation tools. Scale: Successful rollout of core agents used by the entire Pencil user base. This role is responsible for what things look like and why they look that way. You direct and produce work, not just review it. ‍ Why This Role Exists Individual designers do their best work when there is a strong visual direction to work within. Good art direction is the difference between a brand that feels like a specific place and one that feels assembled. ‍ About Little Plains Little Plains is a modern brand and product studio. We work primarily with early-stage companies, often from pre-launch through Series A, at moments where clarity, speed, and conviction matter most. Our partners are founders and small senior teams building new products, categories, and narratives under real constraints. We operate as an embedded creative partner rather than a traditional digital agency, building brand, product, and go-to-market work into cohesive systems that can scale as companies grow. Our work spans healthcare, biotech, SaaS, and consumer products. ‍ What You Will Do Day To Day, You Will Lead visual direction for brand and campaign work on your projects Develop visual concepts and art direction from early exploration through final execution Work closely with brand designers to shape, refine, and strengthen the output Define and extend visual systems across multiple touchpoints, not just hero executions Direct photography, illustration, motion, and typography at a concept and execution level Collaborate with brand strategists and copywriters to make sure visual and verbal expression earn each other Use AI image and generative tools to accelerate visual exploration and concept development Hold a high bar for quality and push work forward when it is not there yet ‍ How We Work Art directors at Little Plains are in the work, not above it. Visual concepts are developed through making, not just briefing. Feedback is specific and tied to the intent of the brand, not personal preference. AI is part of the visual exploration process here. The judgment about what to keep or develop is still yours. ‍ The Bar for Craft This role requires a developed visual point of view, the ability to build coherent brand worlds across executions, and the confidence to push work to a higher standard when it does not meet the brief. ‍ What We're Looking For 3–5 years of art direction experience, at an agency, studio, or in-house A portfolio that demonstrates strong visual point of view across brand, campaign, and digital contexts Ability to develop and hold visual direction across a brand system, not just individual executions Comfort directing multiple disciplines: design, photography, illustration, motion Experience working with early-stage companies or building brands from scratch is a plus Active use of AI generative tools (e.g. Flora, Weavy, MidJourney, Claude, Paper) for visual exploration and concept development Strong collaboration skills and the ability to give direct, useful creative feedback ‍ Benefits (Full-Time) Quarterly profit share tied to studio performance Equity participation for long-term team members Medical, dental, and vision insurance (50% employer-covered) 15 days PTO (Year 1, increases by 2 days/year) One week paid office closure every summer Paid office closure between Christmas and New Year's Parental leave: 12 weeks (birth parent), 6 weeks (non-birth parent) at full pay Prenatal leave: 20 hours paid per year $1,000/year craft and tools stipend $200/mont

The Director, Editorial & Enterprise Storytelling is designed for candidates with a strong editorial or journalism background. title: Creative Strategist — Direct Response Video Ads (Remote)
description: CREATIVE STRATEGIST Direct Response Video Ads | Health & Beauty Remote | Full-Time | US Eastern Hours Opportunity Overview We’re a DTC subscription brand doing ~$2.5M/month across ~45,000 active subscribers. Our products help women with hair, anti-aging, and skincare. We sell primarily through Meta ads, with native, YouTube, TikTok, and Google as secondary channels. Creative is our #1 growth lever. Full stop. We need a Creative Strategist who can produce 13–20 net unique video ad concepts per week, manage the QA pipeline, and help us scale winning creative across four product funnels. This is not a brand advertising role. This is direct response. If you don’t know what a hook rate is, this isn’t for you. If you think “creative strategy” means mood boards and brand guidelines, keep scrolling. What You’ll Actually Do • Write 13–20 video ad scripts per week across multiple product funnels (hair, anti-aging, gummies, skincare). You’ll work alongside one senior strategist who’s currently carrying all four funnels solo. Your job is to take half that load and run with it. Every concept must be a net unique—different format, different angle, different structure. We don’t want five variations of the same talking head. We want a UGC ad, a podcast clip, an AI avatar interview, a skit, an ingredient animation, a street interview—real creative range. • Concept, script, and QA short-form video ads (1–3 minutes) for Meta, native, and YouTube. You own the creative from idea to launch-ready asset. That means spying on what’s working across verticals, pulling inspiration from outside your niche, and building briefs that give editors everything they need to execute. • QA is 50% of this job. Frame-by-frame review of every video before it goes live. You’ll send ads back to editors 2–3 times until they’re perfect. Caption sizing, image mismatches, pacing, transitions, audio sync—nothing ships unless it’s right. If you’re not the type of person who notices a 2px caption offset or a 0.5-second pacing issue, this role will eat you alive. • Manage a team of 3–5 video editors (Philippines and Brazil-based) and coordinate with creator/UGC talent. You give clear briefs with specific format instructions, set deadlines, track revision rates, and hold people accountable to output and quality standards. Editors get lazy—your job is to keep standards high and never let slop through. • Spy relentlessly. The best creative strategists are obsessive about knowing what’s working—not just in our vertical, but across all of direct response. You’re scrolling organic content daily, combing through ad libraries and spy tools, and pulling formats and structures from other verticals to apply to ours. Our best concepts come from finding a validated angle in our space and layering in a format from a completely different vertical. That’s the unlock. • Read performance data and adjust. You’ll have access to ad account dashboards. You need to understand CAC, CPC, CTR, hook rate, and hold rate—and use that data to inform what you write next. If an ad isn’t taking spend, you should be able to diagnose why and pivot the next brief accordingly. • Understand how ad creative connects to funnels. We run advertorial and VSL-style landing pages. The ad’s promise has to match the funnel’s story. You need to understand (or be willing to learn fast) how the full pipeline works from click to conversion. Who We’re Looking For The ideal candidate: •     Has produced winning direct response video ads—ideally in health, supplement, or beauty DTC. You can point to ads you wrote that actually scaled and explain why they worked with real metrics, not vanity numbers. •     Understands hook theory. You know that 90% of ad performance lives in the first 3 seconds. You think hook-first, always. Every brief starts with the hook, not the body copy. •     Knows what a net unique concept is. Every ad you produce should be genuinely different from the last—new formats, new structures, new avatar types, ne

title: Creative Director, Brand Narratives
description: About Pinterest Millions of people around the world come to our platform to find creative ideas, dream about new possibilities and plan for memories that will last a lifetime. At Pinterest, we’re on a mission to bring everyone the inspiration to create a life they love, and that starts with the people behind the product. Discover a career where you ignite innovation for millions, transform passion into growth opportunities, celebrate each other’s unique experiences and embrace the flexibility to do your best work. Creating a career you love? It’s Possible. At Pinterest, AI isn't just a feature, it's a powerful partner that augments our creativity and amplifies our impact, and we’re looking for candidates who are excited to be a part of that. To get a complete picture of your experience and abilities, we’ll explore your foundational skills and how you collaborate with AI. Through our interview process, what matters most is that you can always explain your approach, showing us not just what you know, but how you think. You can read more about our AI interview philosophy and how we use AI in our recruiting process here. Pinterest is a company like no other. And this is a moment like no other. We’ve spent the last decade building one of the world’s most loved platforms. Now we’re telling bigger, bolder, more consequential stories than we ever have—especially to the advertisers and agencies who power our business—and we need someone exceptional to lead them. You’ll be the creative director behind Pinterest’s most important advertiser narratives: the keynotes that land on the world’s biggest stages, the thought leadership that shifts how marketers think about our platform, the executive communications that give our leaders a distinct and credible voice with CMOs and agency heads, and the B2B campaigns and sales narratives that move real business. This is a leadership role for someone who thinks like a strategist, writes like a pro, and knows how to build a function that scales across Business Marketing, Sales, and Comms. You’re right for this role if you’ve built the narrative frameworks that shape how a company talks about itself to the market. You’ve stood in front of a CMO and held your own. You’ve turned a research insight into a keynote that changed minds—and into sales materials that changed plans. And you’re ready to do all of that at Pinterest, where storytelling has always been a competitive advantage and B2B narratives are now a primary growth lever. What You’ll Do Own Pinterest’s Brand Narratives function within the marketing org: setting the creative vision, quality bar, and strategic direction for our highest-impact work: event keynotes, executive messaging, advertiser positioning, industry thought leadership, and scaled B2B content. Lead the end-to-end development of Pinterest’s biggest storytelling moments, including Pinterest Presents (our flagship advertiser summit), Pinterest Predicts (our annual trend report and campaign), and major tentpole events that reach thousands of clients, press, and industry leaders. Partner directly with C-suite and senior executives to develop their thought leadership voice. You’ll write speeches, talking points, and narratives that land on stages around the world, and you’ll know how to listen, steer, and hold your ground when the story needs defending. Drive Pinterest’s B2B messaging strategy in close partnership with Product Marketing, Business Marketing, Research, and Legal. You’ll develop the frameworks that earn company-wide adoption, and make sure they actually get used. Partner with Creative Directors, writers, designers, and producers across House of Creative to bring narratives to life across formats: decks, films, event experiences, digital content, and social—always with advertisers as the primary audience. Lead and develop a team of senior writers. You’ll set the creative culture, establish ways of working, and scale your own editorial judgment across everything the team ma
score_reason: Senior creative director role at major tech company with AI integration aligns with Travis's background, but missing salary transparency, work location flexibility explicitly stated, and posting cuts off mid-description—can't fully assess scope.

Month 12. Full team in place. Response times held under SLA. Review-score floor hit. Refund dollars inside guardrail. The CEO has not answered a guest ticket in six months. What You'll Need A guest-ops or service system you personally built from zero. SLA, escalation, QA, something. Bring the artifact and the number it moved. The build matters more than the résumé. LLMs building your knowledge base and automating your responses, with prompts you can show. Not "I've used ChatGPT." Management reps: people you've hired, coached, and let go,

title: Chief Strategy and Growth Officer
description: About Cinereach For two decades, Cinereach has been a home for visionary storytellers and groundbreaking ideas in independent film. We work at the intersection of storytelling and the systems around it, helping creators and narrative practitioners navigate AI, emerging distribution platforms, shifting economics, and changing ecosystems. The Opportunity Cinereach is seeking a Chief Strategy and Growth Officer to help lead the organization into its next chapter. Reporting to the CEO and serving as a member of the executive team, this role leads Cinereach’s strategic direction and growth agenda, with Partnerships, Business Development, and Brand and Marketing reporting into it. This is not a traditional strategic planning role and it is not a role for someone whose contribution ends at the framework. Cinereach already has deep strategic thinkers, strong foresight instincts, and rich cultural and narrative expertise across the organization. What this role adds is the ability to turn insight into action: to translate where the field is moving into partnerships that get struck, new revenue that gets built, and a brand and community that grow measurably. The work spans three connected dimensions. Strategic direction means using foresight, market analysis, and cultural and narrative analysis to chart where things are moving and where Cinereach should place its bets. New revenue and business development means building toward real revenue, from partnerships to potential service lines for creators and narrative infrastructure, including up to one-third of revenue from new sources within two to three years. And activation means turning all of it into execution rather than frameworks alone. Key Responsibilities Strategic Direction & Positioning: Drive and refine strategic direction based on cultural intelligence tools, market analysis, foresight, and ecosystem signals. Develop strategic insight and planning frameworks, and decision tools that guide leadership choices across the organization. Clarify Cinereach’s positioning in the broader landscape: where we are uniquely suited to lead, where partnership is the better path, and what we should stop, evolve, or sunset. Formalize the planning and insights function so external signals are regularly translated into actionable choices for leadership and CEO decision-making. Growth, Business Development & New Revenue: Own a growth agenda that translates into measurable new revenue, including up to one-third of revenue from new sources within two to three years. Identify, test, and launch mission-aligned growth tracks including partnerships, content offerings, and service models for creators, that deepen relevance and support long-term organizational health. Lead partnerships where they decidedly become business development: co-created ventures, transactions, and revenue-bearing relationships. Think holistically about the pipeline across Cinereach’s revenue lines, philanthropy, fund and investment activity, and new service models, so the organization can sequence and resource its bets. Brand, Communications & Thought Leadership: Lead the next phase of Cinereach’s brand and communications strategy, ensuring messaging, positioning, audience strategy, and marketing funnels support both mission and growth. Build a thought leadership platform that strengthens Cinereach’s influence in the field, turning insight into visibility, trust, and strategic differentiation. Oversee how Cinereach communicates what it uniquely offers and why it matters now, across creators, partners, funders, and broader audiences. Community, Learning & Activation: Build and deepen relationships with key constituencies, creators, storytellers, partners, f

Hands-on experience with generative image, video, and copy tools, including prompt design, iteration strategy, references, and QA. Proven ability to create work that is commercially usable, not just technically interesting. Experience creating or supporting creative testing for paid media, ecommerce, or growth channels. Ability to personally produce or direct photoreal creative output that minimizes obvious AI artifacts. Strong builder mentality: you like shipping, refining, and proving systems in real operating environments. Preferred Qualifications Experience with Meta creative testing, paid social experimentation, or performance creative operations. Experience building audience-specific variant systems for advertising or lifecycle marketing. Experience in ecommerce, retail, lifestyle, home, fashion, or design-led consumer brands. Familiarity with automation tooling, APIs, structured prompts, and workflow orchestration. Experience teaching creative or marketing teams how to use AI systems in practice. Why Join The people who do best here are builders. They take ownership, move fast, and want to see the direct impact of their work. Cross-Functional Impact: Your work ships into live growth systems across 13 brands—every asset and work

title: Responsable, image de marque

Reconnu mondialement pour ses importantes contributions au domaine de l'apprentissage profond, Mila s'est particulièrement distingué dans la modélisation du langage, la traduction automatique, la reconnaissance d'objets et les modèles génératifs. Depuis 2017, Mila est le fruit d'une collaboration entre l'Université de Montréal et l'Université McGill, en lien étroit avec Polytechnique Montréal et HEC Montréal. Mila s'est donné pour mission d'être un pôle mondial d'avancées scientifiques qui inspire l'innovation et l'essor de l'intelligence artificielle (IA) au bénéfice de tous. Pour en connaitre davantage, veuillez consulter https://mila.quebec/ Sommaire du poste Mila invite un·e Responsable du Design de Marque à rejoindre son équipe pour faire rayonner l'identité visuelle de notre organisation. Au sein d'un collectif dynamique et en plein essor, vous jouerez un rôle clé pour incarner et faire évoluer l'image de Mila, référence mondiale en IA. Ce rôle repose sur une alliance entre vision artistique inspirante, rigueur créativité et curiosité pour les nouvelles frontières de l'intelligence artificielle générative. Nous recherchons une personne prête à co-construire l'avenir visuel de Mila avec nous. Vos responsabilités Vision globale et leadership de marque Direction artistique : Piloter la direction artistique de Mila en veillant à l'alignement de chaque concept visuel avec la marque établie et les objectifs stratégiques de l'organisation Identité de marque : Décliner et maintenir une identité de marque puissante, cohérente et distinctive, capable de positionner l'organisation comme un leader incontournable en matière d'IA à l'échelle mondiale Conception d'actifs et innovation 360° Création d'actifs d'exception : Donner vie à la stratégie de communication par la création de visuels percutants pour des campagnes publicitaires, du design numérique et des supports événementiels Expérience de marque : Enrichir l'expérience de marque à travers chaque point de contact, physique ou numérique, en repoussant les frontières conventionnelles du design graphique Rigueur d'exécution et excellence méthodologique Qualité irréprochable : Assurer une précision graphique et technique totale, de la phase de recherche et d'idéation jusqu'à la production finale Adhésion stratégique : Présenter vos concepts de manière structurée et convaincante afin de garantir l'alignement et l'adhésion des parties prenantes aux projets les plus ambitieux Curiosité, exploration technique et avant-garde IA Innovation et optimisation des processus : Incarner l'ADN de Mila en explorant et en intégrant de manière proactive les outils d'IA générative et les techniques de pointe dans la démarche créative. Requirements Profil Recherché Diplôme universitaire en design graphique ou formation équivalente Expérience : minimum 10 à 12 ans en design graphique ou direction artistique (idéalement en agence, dans un environnement très créatif ou auprès de marques avec un ADN fort en design) Maîtrise technique : Maîtrise experte d'Adobe et des outils de design actuels Figma, etc Expertise IA : Curiosité marquée et expérience pratique avec les outils de création assistée par IA (Midjourney, Nano Banana, Veo 3, Flora, Claude Cowork) Portfolio : Un portfolio solide démontrant une capacité à gérer des identités de marque complexes et des déploiements 360° Aptitudes : Grande rigueur, capacité à travailler sous des délais serrés et esprit collaboratif Créativité et Polyvalence Gestion de collaborateurs internes et externes Être bilingue, en raison des interactions que vous aurez dans le cadre de votre emploi avec certains de nos partenaires, parties prenantes, ou membres de notre communauté académique anglophones Benefits De bonne

Orchestrate large-scale creator campaigns and earned media pushes to establish Doctours as a leader in the healthcare space. The ideal candidate Has a proven track record of growing independent publications or social communities to tens of thousands of subscribers. Combines 2-3 years at a top-tier consultancy (McKinsey, BCG, or Bain) with experience shipping creative multi-six-figure marketing campaigns. Possesses exceptional writing skills and the operator mindset required to translate abstract narratives into tangible growth and culture. Who are Jack & Jill? Ok, I'll go first. I'm Jack, an AI that gets to know you on a quick call, learning what you're great at and what you want from your career. Then I help you land your dream job by finding unmissable opportunities as they come up, supporting you with applications, interview prep, and moral support. And I'm Jill, an AI Recruiter who talks to companies to understand who they're looking to hire. Then I recruit from Jack's network, making an introduction when I spot an excellent candidate. How does this work? Jack's an AI agent for job searching and career coaching. He works for you. Jill is the AI recruiter working for the company. She recruits from Jack's network. If it's a match and the company wants to meet you, they'll make the intro. In the meantime, if you'd like, Jack will send you excellent alternatives. We never post fake jobs This isn't a trick. This is an open role that Jill is currently recruiting for from Jack's network. Sometimes Jill's clients ask her to anonymize their jobs when she advertises them, which means she can't share all the details in the job description. We appreciate this can make them look a bit suspect, but there isn't much we can do about it. Give Jack a spin! You could land this role. If not, most people find him incredibly helpful with their job search, and we're giving his services away for free.

You have shipped both: real creative work (motion, design, video, 3D, photography, brand systems) AND real technical work (apps, integrations, plugins, automation). • Creative content supply chain fluency. You understand how briefs become assets, how brand systems and design tokens work, how master files become channel variants, how creative teams hand off through Figma / PSD / INDD to DAM and review-and-approval. • Firefly Services + Workflow Builder fluency — or evidence you ramp on a new API surface in days, not months. Adobe ecosystem depth (AEM, Workfront, Frame.io, App Builder) is a strong plus, as is Figma plugin / API fluency. • Vibe-coding with judgment. You use Cursor / Claude Code / Copilot daily and ship fast. You also know when AI output is wrong, when error handling is missing, when the prompt needs more context. Production code, not prototype-only. • Extreme ownership and a GSD attitude. When the integration breaks before a CMO demo, you fix it — you don't escalate. You own outcomes end-to-end, from broken auth to a missed brand-compliance check. You ship ugly first and refuse to let process slow you down. • Extreme customer orientation. You hold the room with CMOs, creative directors, and heads of IT. You hear a customer pain and respond with a credible, impact-focused, creative solution. You translate tech to ROI without losing the engineers in the room. • A great nose for the money. You instinctively connect what you build to business outcomes (ie: time-to-activation, cost per variant, brand-compliance pass rate, campaign velocity.) • A passion for coding and tinkering. A deep interest in generative AI and creative workflows. You explore adjacent orchestration tools — ComfyUI for visual workflow prototyping, n8n / Zapier / Power Automate for low-code connectors, LangGraph / LangChain for agentic flows. • A public portfolio — link or PDF at application. Must show shipped creative work AND shipped technical artifacts. This is a hard requirement; applications without a portfolio will not be reviewed. What we're not looking for: • Backend-only or distributed-systems engineers with no creative practitioner background. • Pure designers or creatives who can't stand up a working integration. • ML / AI researchers who are not interested in customer outcomes. • PMs / consultants who don't ship. Every team member writes code AND produces creative work.

Significant agency experience strongly preferred Deep expertise in omnichannel strategy, customer journey design, CRM, personalization, and customer engagement planning Strong understanding of commercial pharmaceutical marketing and the unique dynamics between commercial, medical affairs, and medical communications functions Experience supporting pharmaceutical brands across launch, growth, lifecycle management, and customer engagement initiatives Demonstrated ability to interpret data and analytics and translate findings into meaningful business recommendations and engagement strategies Strong track record of success supporting business development efforts, including RFI/RFP responses, strategic proposals, and client pitch presentations Strong executive presence with the ability to engage and influence senior client stakeholders and significant experience working directly with clients including willingness to travel for client meetings, workshops, conferences, and business development activities as needed Familiarity with AI-enabled platforms, generative AI tools, customer engagement technologies, and emerging applications of AI within healthcare communications and marketing Confident abilities as a compelling storyteller with exceptional presentation, facilitation, and strategic thinking skills Location - New York metropolitan area preferred, with the ability to travel regularly to

title: Head of Retention
description: About the Company We are partnering exclusively with one of the fastest-growing consumer health companies in the world. The brand is subscription-first, premium DTC, and already operating at nine-figure ARR across 40+ countries — all achieved in under two years. It is backed by serious institutional capital and high-profile brand and athlete partnerships. Retention is now the primary growth lever. The function has not been built at this scale yet. This role builds it from the ground up. About the Role This is a full subscriber P&L ownership role — not a CRM manager position with a senior title. You will own every metric that drives long-term subscriber value: NRR, gross churn, LTV, payback period, and reactivation rate. You will build the lifecycle programme, the cross-sell engine, the AI-native operating model, and the loyalty framework that makes this brand's subscriber relationship category-defining. Key responsibilities include: Owning subscriber P&L — NRR, gross and voluntary churn, LTV, reactivation rate, and payback Driving monthly-to-annual subscription migration and building dunning and payment retry flows Running lifecycle and CRM end-to-end across email, SMS, push, and in-app in 40+ markets Leading a lifecycle creative team to high-volume, high-quality output Building an AI-native operating model — predictive churn modelling, generative content at scale, agentic workflows Architecting cross-sell engines that turn single-SKU buyers into multi-product subscribers Building loyalty, referral, and community programmes that compound Delivering cohort analytics, LTV forecasting, and weekly retention reporting Partnering with performance marketing, product, and finance on LTV signals and subscriber economics What We're Looking For The following are non-negotiable: ✓ Scale credentials — $50M+ ARR or 250,000+ active subscribers personally owned. Or a clear, specific case for why this benchmark does not apply to your trajectory. ✓ AI-native — not AI-curious. LLMs, predictive models, and generative tools are already embedded in your daily workflow and you can demonstrate the output. ✓ Demonstrable retention impact — specific and defensible movement in NRR, gross churn, or LTV with a clear story of how you achieved it. ✓ Platform depth — hands-on expertise in Klaviyo, Attentive, Recharge/Skio/Stay, Shopify, and the data infrastructure connecting them. ✓ Subscription DTC fluency — dunning, pause vs. cancel logic, monthly-to-annual offer architecture, replenishment cadence, win-back economics. ✓ Creative leadership — you can brief a winning email and identify a weak one in five seconds. ✓ Commercial range — cohort analysis, LTV modelling, and payback math. You can defend your numbers to a CFO. Advantageous (not required): Background in health, wellness, supplements, beauty, or premium lifestyle DTC Experience scaling retention across multiple international markets Loyalty programme design and execution at scale Community, content, or membership-led retention experience A clear point of view on where retention is heading in the next 24 months
score_reason: Senior DTC leadership with AI-native creative scope and full P&L ownership, but retention/CRM focus is adjacent to Travis's brand/campaign DNA—worth exploring if remote is confirmed.
